source: NvChad - NvChad UI: Plugins Manager: update/sync/profile/log/debug/restore and the lock file

questions.q: In :Lazy, which key checks for updates?
questions.why: C runs git fetch and lists updatable plugins.

questions.q: Which key updates all checked plugins at once?
questions.why: U updates all; u updates an individually selected plugin.

questions.q: The Sync operation (:Lazy sync / S) is a concatenation of...
questions.why: Sync runs install, clean and update together.

options: plugin loading times (a profiler for troubleshooting slowness)
questions.why: The profiler helps find slow plugins.

questions.q: Which key/command shows the last operations performed on plugins?
questions.why: L opens the log of recent plugin operations.

options: active lazy-loading handlers and the module cache
questions.why: Debug shows loading handlers and what is cached.

questions.q: What does the lazy-lock.json file enable?
options: Syncing/restoring the exact plugin commit state across machines
questions.why: It records each plugin's branch and commit.

questions.q: The Restore function (R / :Lazy restore) does what?
options: Returns all plugins to the state recorded in lazy-lock.json
questions.why: Restore brings plugins back to the locked commits.
